title: Europe seeks to defuse Trump criticism ahead of NATO summit in Ankara
subhead: Disoriented by a flurry of announcements regarding the stationing of US troops on the continent, European foreign ministers sought to convince Marco Rubio of their efforts to take greater responsibility for collective defense and to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Five arrested over smuggling of Californian cannabis to France
subhead: A former influencer known as 'PA7' and a counterfeiter known as 'the Prince of Fake Luxury Watches' are suspected of having illegally imported hundreds of kilograms of 'Cali' marijuana.

title: Paris left splits over awarding of city's marathon to Bolloré-linked company
subhead: The Cadence group – which notably includes Havas, owned by billionaire Vincent Bolloré – will organize the next four Paris marathons and half-marathons, the Paris City Council decided on Wednesday.

title: Cannes: 'Everytime' by Sandra Wollner picks up the Un Certain Regard Prize
subhead: The competition of 18 feature films was marked by stories exploring the maternal bond and the disappearance of young girls.

title: Cubans on the specter of US intervention: 'On what grounds would a foreign government have the right to decide for us?'
subhead: In Havana, the possibility of a US intervention has once again become a topic of conversation. Cuba's civil defense has shared a guide explaining how families can protect themselves in case of a 'military attack.'

Iran is considering taxing users of undersea cables in the Strait of Hormuz
subhead: After blocking ship traffic, Iran is threatening to target the powerful undersea fiber-optic arteries that cross through the maritime chokepoint. These cables are essential to the smooth functioning of the internet and sensitive sectors such as energy and banking.

title: Coordinator of 2015 Paris attacks could be released from prison early under Belgian law
subhead: Sentenced in France for his role in the terrorist attacks that killed 132 people on November 13, 2015, Mohamed Bakkali was, in theory, not eligible for release under French law before 2035. But in Belgium, where he is incarcerated, he benefits from a significantly more lenient sentencing regime.

title: France remains top destination for foreign investment as Europe declines
subhead: France attracted the most foreign investment in Europe in 2025, according to the EY barometer published on Thursday, despite an overall decline in the number of projects and associated jobs across the continent.
